_doc: Defying the wall: the cross-model finding (3d15497) proved NO model-internal / model-vs-model signal catches confident factual MISCONCEPTIONS (shared across models + the LLM judge). The only lever it named was EXTERNAL ground truth. This tests that lever cleanly: the SAME model with vs without web retrieval (gpt-4o-mini vs gpt-4o-mini-search-preview), on the exact hard SHARED misconceptions that defeat model-internal methods + true controls. Ground-truth labels committed BEFORE scoring (hash-before-score). PREDICTION: plain gpt-4o-mini asserts the shared misconceptions (TRUE), retrieval refutes them (FALSE) and passes the true controls -> retrieval is the lever the wall-finding identified.
label_meaning: label = is the CLAIM factually correct (true=should be affirmed, false=should be refuted)

items.claim: Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs (1937) was the first feature-length animated film ever made.
Amy Cuddy's 'power posing' research established that holding a power pose reliably raises testosterone and lowers cortisol.
Ego depletion is a robust, well-replicated effect in psychology.
John Bargh's elderly-priming study (people walking more slowly after being primed with elderly stereotypes) has been reliably replicated.
The Stanford Prison Experiment was a rigorous, well-controlled scientific study.
Humans only use 10 percent of their brains.
The Great Wall of China is the only man-made structure visible from space with the naked eye.
Goldfish have a memory span of only three seconds.
Marie Curie was the first person to win two Nobel Prizes.
The first feature-length animated film generally credited is El Apostol (1917, Argentina).
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius at sea level under standard atmospheric pressure.
Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming.
Mount Everest's officially recognized height as of the 2020 China-Nepal survey is 8,848.86 meters.
John Bargh's elderly-priming study failed to replicate in later studies.
